title: Service Log Export — Diagnosis & Report Spec

evidence_term_checklist.matching: dossier_quote and repair_action terms are case-insensitive; pipeline_evidence terms are case-sensitive and the complete evidence value must be a literal substring of the frozen source

repair_audit_field_shapes.patched_workflow: plain JSON string equal to /app/workflow/export_report.py; never an object
repair_audit_field_shapes.post_repair: object with integer flagged_count and rerun_flagged_count

quick_contract_index.processing_steps_contract: copy repair_audit.processing_steps verbatim, preserving exact strings and order

absolute_policy_sources.resolution: --input replaces only events_default; policy paths remain absolute and are never resolved relative to --input or --output-dir

quick_contract_index.internal_python_api: not prescribed; helper function names, parameters, and return values are implementation details

repair.output_dir_contract: all five repaired artifacts must be written under the provided --output-dir path; when a custom path is supplied, do not create, rewrite, or update any artifact under /app/output, including /app/output/diagnosis.json

evidence.pipeline_evidence_format: literal substring excerpt from frozen original workflow source
evidence.term_matching: dossier_quote and repair_action term checks are case-insensitive; pipeline_evidence term checks are case-sensitive literal substrings

input_stats.exact_key_names: input_stats keys must be exactly event_count, unique_event_ids, services
input_stats.services_normalization: services must use the same canonicalization as canonicalization.service_normalization (strip/lower + aliases); raw unnormalized spellings are invalid

verified_summary.completeness_contract: verified_summary must be a full key-for-key copy of summary.json in repair mode; omissions (for example raw_event_count) are invalid

output_paths.key_naming_contract: keys are semantic snake_case names, not filenames with dots

diagnosis_report.top_level_key_policy: required_keys_diagnosed and required_keys_repaired are EXACT top-level key sets, not minimums. Do not add extra top-level keys (for example a separate `status` field alongside pipeline_status) in either mode.
diagnosis_report.pipeline_status_note: pipeline_status values are exact short literals: `diagnosed` after diagnose, `repaired` after repair. Do not substitute descriptive sentences.

canonicalization.dedupe_by_id: collapse duplicate event ids keeping the row with highest ts_ms; if ts_ms ties, keep higher severity rank (error>warn>info>debug); if still tied, prefer non-suppressed row; if still tied, keep lexicographically greater normalized message; if still tied, keep lexicographically greater normalized service
canonicalization.level_normalization: normalize levels with str(level).strip().lower() before counting and flagging
canonicalization.service_normalization: normalize services with str(service).strip().lower() and apply aliases: api-gw->api, api gateway->api, database->db, worker-batch->worker
canonicalization.ts_ms_normalization: coerce ts_ms with int(str(ts_ms).strip()); invalid values become 0
canonicalization.message_normalization: collapse internal whitespace in message using split/join normalization before tie-breaks and output
canonicalization.suppressed_normalization: coerce suppressed booleans from bools and strings (true/1/yes => true, false/0/no/empty => false)
canonicalization.silence_level_scope_normalization: silence level_scope uses str(...).strip().lower(); supported values are all, warn, error
canonicalization.silence_level_scope_filter_rule: drop silence rows whose normalized level_scope is not one of {all, warn, error} before interval compaction and checksum computation
canonicalization.silence_window_canonicalization: silence windows come from /app/data/silence_windows.json; normalize service aliases and level_scope, coerce start_ms/end_ms with int(str(...).strip()), drop rows where end_ms <= start_ms, compact overlap/touch per (service, level_scope)
canonicalization.silence_compaction_merge_rule: when compacting sorted silence intervals for a key, merge if next.start_ms <= current.end_ms (touching intervals must merge). An equivalent implementation starts a new interval only when next.start_ms > current.end_ms; the '>' branch does not mean touching intervals remain separate.

notes: api gateway and api-gw normalize to api, and their touching/overlapping warn intervals merge to [1700000008500,1700000008700)
debug scope is dropped because it is unsupported
search start_ms='oops' coerces to 0, so [0,10) is valid and retained

canonicalization.silence_match_rule: an event is silenced when start_ms <= ts_ms < end_ms for same normalized service and level_scope in {all, event.level}
canonicalization.canonical_order: after dedupe, canonical rows are sorted by ts_ms ascending (stable for ties)
canonicalization.total_events: count canonical deduped events (suppressed rows remain in totals)
canonicalization.flagged_export: include warn and error only, exclude suppressed=true, exclude silenced rows by silence_match_rule, then compute stateful dependency pressure and the single authoritative event_digest for included rows; final sort is defined by flagged_sort_tiebreaks
canonicalization.silence_pressure_score: for each included flagged row, compute probe window [ts_ms-90, ts_ms+1) overlap against compacted (service, all) and (service, level) silence windows; score=(all_overlap_ms//25)+(level_overlap_ms//15)
canonicalization.event_digest: first 10 lowercase SHA1 hex chars of id|ts_ms|level|service|message|silence_pressure_score|dependency_pressure_score|comma_joined_dependency_source_ids|dependency_chain_depth|causal_burst_score|comma_joined_dependency_lineage_ids|lineage_digest|blast_radius_score|comma_joined_blast_radius_services|blast_radius_digest; this is the only event_digest definition

event_digest_worked_example.delimiter_contract: literal pipe between fields, comma inside ordered lists, no trailing delimiter

canonicalization.flagged_sort_tiebreaks: ts_ms desc, severity rank desc, causal_burst_score desc, blast_radius_score desc, dependency_pressure_score desc, dependency_chain_depth desc, silence_pressure_score desc, id asc

patched_workflow_contract.not_allowed: object, map, or nested path descriptor

removed_tokens_schema.description: Map each forbidden_executable_tokens entry to a boolean (true when absent from patched executable code, excluding comments and docstrings).
removed_tokens_schema.required_key_source: keys must be the exact literal strings from forbidden_executable_tokens (no semantic aliases)

pre_repair.field_names_contract: use these names exactly; frozen_source, sha256, source_sha256, or other aliases are invalid
pre_repair.capture_rule: pipeline_source_sha256 and pipeline_tokens_present are computed from the frozen original snapshot bytes before applying any patch to /app/workflow/export_report.py

pipeline_tokens_present_schema.description: Map each forbidden_executable_tokens entry to a boolean (true when present in frozen broken snapshot before repair).
pipeline_tokens_present_schema.required_key_source: keys must be the exact literal strings from forbidden_executable_tokens (no semantic aliases)

post_repair.field_contract: flagged_count is summary.json.flagged_count from the repaired run; rerun_flagged_count is the second idempotency run's summary.json.flagged_count

workflow_repair.public_contract: only the CLI and generated files are contractual; an internal export_report function is optional, and no internal function signature or return type is prescribed

repair_state_contract.self_contained_invocation: every invocation of `python3 /app/log_audit.py repair --output-dir PATH` must install a correct repaired implementation at /app/workflow/export_report.py before executing it
repair_state_contract.starting_state: the current /app/workflow/export_report.py may contain the frozen broken implementation, a previous repair, or arbitrary stale contents; repair must not assume solve.sh or an earlier repair already patched it
repair_state_contract.reset_scenario: verification may copy /app/workflow/.export_report.original over /app/workflow/export_report.py immediately before invoking repair, including when a custom --output-dir is used
required_sequence: read and capture pre-repair evidence from /app/workflow/.export_report.original
write or generate the repaired executable at /app/workflow/export_report.py
run that repaired executable with the requested output directory
run it again for idempotency verification
repair_state_contract.output_directory_rule: --output-dir changes where the five output artifacts are written; it does not change the workflow path that must be repaired
repair_state_contract.custom_output_isolation: when --output-dir is not /app/output, write no files under /app/output and leave every pre-existing default-output artifact byte-for-byte unchanged

workflow_repair.implementation_freedom: variable names, constants, helper structure, and sorting syntax are not contractual; verification asserts generated behavior and files
workflow_repair.forbidden_token_check_scope: executable code only (exclude comments and docstrings)

summary_json.canonical_fingerprint: sha1 over canonical rows in canonical_order joined by newline where each row is 'id|ts_ms|level|service|message|suppressed_int' and suppressed_int is 1 or 0
summary_json.silence_excluded_count: number of canonical warn/error rows excluded by silence windows (after suppressed exclusion)
summary_json.silence_compaction_checksum: sha256 over compacted silence rows in canonical order (service, level_scope, start_ms, end_ms) as lines service|level_scope|start_ms|end_ms
summary_json.dependency_compaction_checksum: sha256 over canonical dependency rules in order (upstream_service, downstream_service, lag_min_ms, lag_max_ms), as newline-joined upstream_service|downstream_service|lag_min_ms|lag_max_ms|weight
summary_json.max_silence_pressure_score: maximum silence_pressure_score across final flagged rows (0 when there are no flagged rows)
summary_json.max_dependency_pressure_score: maximum dependency_pressure_score across final flagged rows (0 when there are no flagged rows)
summary_json.max_causal_burst_score: maximum causal_burst_score across final flagged rows (0 when there are no flagged rows)
summary_json.max_dependency_chain_depth: maximum dependency_chain_depth across final flagged rows (0 when there are no flagged rows)
summary_json.max_blast_radius_score: maximum blast_radius_score across final flagged rows (0 when there are no flagged rows)
summary_json.blast_radius_digest_checksum: sha256('|'.join(blast_radius_digest values in final flagged order)) lowercase 64 hex chars
summary_json.lineage_digest_checksum: sha256('|'.join(lineage_digest values in final flagged order)) lowercase 64 hex chars
summary_json.flagged_digest_checksum: sha256('|'.join(event_digest values in final flagged order)) lowercase 64 hex chars

service_matrix_json.description: The JSON root is the service map itself, with no schema_version field, services wrapper, metadata wrapper, or other envelope. Each top-level key is a canonical service name and each value is that service's level-count object.
service_matrix_json.top_level_shape: {canonical_service: {debug: int, error: int, info: int, warn: int}}

flagged_jsonl.sort: ts_ms descending with tiebreaks severity rank desc, causal_burst_score desc, blast_radius_score desc, dependency_pressure_score desc, dependency_chain_depth desc, silence_pressure_score desc, id asc

service_dependencies.canonicalization: normalize both services with canonical service aliases; int-coerce lag and weight fields; drop empty services, lag_min_ms < 0, lag_max_ms <= lag_min_ms, or weight <= 0; dedupe identical (upstream_service, downstream_service, lag_min_ms, lag_max_ms) rules by maximum weight
service_dependencies.candidate_events: dependency sources and targets are final unsuppressed, unsilenced warn/error rows; a source is eligible only when source.ts_ms < target.ts_ms
service_dependencies.rule_match: a canonical rule matches when source.service=upstream_service, target.service=downstream_service, and lag_min_ms <= target.ts_ms-source.ts_ms < lag_max_ms
service_dependencies.evaluation_order: first build the complete eligible unsuppressed/unsilenced warn+error row set; sort it by (ts_ms ascending, id ascending); initialize and finalize each target's dependency fields in that order, so a finalized earlier row's dependency_pressure_score is available when it acts as a source for any later row
service_dependencies.source_contribution: for each distinct earlier source event, evaluate every matching canonical rule and retain exactly one contribution: the maximum of weight*severity_rank(source.level)+source.silence_pressure_score+(source.dependency_pressure_score//2); do not sum multiple matching rules for the same source
service_dependencies.strongest_source_selection: sort contributing sources by contribution descending, source ts_ms descending, source id ascending; retain the first three
service_dependencies.dependency_source_ids: retained source ids in strongest_source_selection order
service_dependencies.dependency_lineage_evaluation: after dependency_source_ids are finalized for a target, visit those direct sources in dependency_source_ids order. Each source has already been finalized because evaluation is chronological.
service_dependencies.dependency_lineage_ids: start empty; for each direct source in order, visit [source.id] followed by that source's dependency_lineage_ids in stored order, append only first occurrences, and stop globally after five ids. Never reorder the resulting list.
service_dependencies.dependency_chain_depth: 0 when dependency_source_ids is empty; otherwise min(1 + max(dependency_chain_depth of the retained direct sources), 6)
service_dependencies.source_burst_rollup: sum source.causal_burst_score // (rank+2) for retained direct sources, where rank is zero-based dependency_source_ids order (divisors 2,3,4)
service_dependencies.lineage_digest: first 12 lowercase SHA256 hex chars of id|dependency_chain_depth|causal_burst_score|comma_joined_dependency_lineage_ids
service_dependencies.blast_radius_graph: collapse canonical dependency rules to one directed service edge per (upstream_service,downstream_service), retaining the maximum weight across all lag ranges
service_dependencies.blast_radius_paths: for each flagged row's service, enumerate every directed path of length one or two. A destination's path capacity is the first-edge weight for length one or min(first-edge weight,second-edge weight) for length two. Exclude paths whose destination is the origin service.
service_dependencies.blast_radius_cycle_and_dedupe: cycles are bounded by the two-hop limit; for each distinct destination retain only its maximum path capacity across all one-hop and two-hop paths
service_dependencies.blast_radius_score: sum the retained maximum path capacity for each blast_radius_services destination
service_dependencies.blast_radius_digest: first 10 lowercase SHA1 hex chars of service|comma_joined_blast_radius_services|blast_radius_score
service_dependencies.event_digest_reference: use canonicalization.event_digest; there is no baseline/extended digest variant

canonical_rules: api->worker lag [50,200) weight 4 (a duplicate weight-2 rule was discarded)

rows: a: api/error has dependency score 0, depth 0, lineage [], burst 0
b: worker/error receives 4*4+0+(0//2)=16 from a; depth 1; lineage [a]; burst=min(16+(0//2)+3,9999)=19
c: db/error receives 2*4+0+(16//2)=16 from b; depth 2; lineage [b,a]; burst=min(16+(19//2)+6,9999)=31
worked_example_recursive_propagation.clarification: b's finalized dependency, depth, lineage, and burst values are used when b contributes to c; rows are not computed independently from an all-zero snapshot

service_dependencies.blast_radius_origin_exclusion: The origin service is never its own blast-radius destination: discard one-hop self-loops and any two-hop path that returns to the origin (a->b->a). blast_radius_services never contains the flagged row's own service.
